data.title: Shell Indonesia to transfer gas stations operations to Citadel-Sefas venture
data.description:
Shell Indonesia, a wholly owned subsidiary of European oil and gas major Shell (NYSE:SHEL), has agreed to transfer ownership of its fuel filling station business in Indonesia. 

The buyer will be a new joint venture between Citadel Pacific and Sefas Group.

Shell's gas station business operations will continue as usual until the completion of the transfer of ownership, which is expected to occur next year.

Once the transfer of ownership is complete, the Shell (NYSE:SHEL) brand will remain in Indonesia through a brand licensing agreement, the company said in a statement.

Indonesia remains a key growth market for Shell’s lubricants business, where it operates a lubricants plant with a capacity of 300 million litres per annum and is building a grease manufacturing plant in Marunda that will have a capacity of 12 kilotonnes per annum.

data.title: Asian indexes climb as Treasury yields ease
data.description:
Asian indexes traded higher on Friday as U.S. Treasury yields eased after a rocky week due to worries in the bond market over mounting U.S. government debt.

Japan (NKY:IND) up 0.43% to 37,145, reversing losses from the previous session as investors reacted to the latest inflation report.

Japan’s core consumer price index, which excludes volatile fresh food prices but includes energy, rose 3.5% Y/Y in April 2025, up from 3.2% in March and slightly above market expectations of 3.4%. 

Japan’s 10-year government bond yield held steady around 1.56% on Friday, lingering near its highest level since 2008

China (SHCOMP) up 0.08% to around 3,383. recouping losses from the previous session as sentiment around Sino-US trade negotiations improved. 

The People’s Bank of China (PBoC) injected CNY 500 billion into financial institutions through a one-year medium-term lending facility (MLF) on May 23rd to maintain ample liquidity in the country's banking system. 

Beijing and Washington maintained high-level contact with a call on Thursday between U.S. Deputy Secretary of State Christopher Landau and China’s Executive Vice Foreign Minister Ma Zhaoxu, signaling ongoing communication following their recent trade truce. 

Hong Kong (HSI) climbed 0.58% to 23,679, swinging from the previous session’s drop.

Malaysia’s leading economic index edged down 0.04% month-on-month in March

India (SENSEX) up 0.69% to 81,506, partly recovering from the previous day's decline amid gains across almost all sectors, led by tech stocks.

Australia's (AS51) S&P/ASX 200 gained 0.14% t 8,360, recouping losses from the previous session, with financial and uranium stocks leading the rebound. 

Wall Street's major averages ended largely flat on Thursday, while a bruising bond sell-off from the previous session eased up. The focus remained on President Trump's "One Big Beautiful" tax bill, which was narrowly passed by the U.S. House.

U.S. stock futures traded in the red on Friday: Dow -0.04%; S&P 500 -0.04%; Nasdaq -0.09%.

data.title: Japan's core inflation rises to 3.5% in April
Xerox slashes dividend for the second time to $0.025, reiterates FY25 guidance
data.description:
• Xerox (NASDAQ:XRX) declares $0.025/share quarterly dividend, -80% decrease from prior dividend of $0.125. In the last quarter, the company had lowered the dividend by 50% to $0.125, implying 90% cut from Oct 2024 dividend of $0.25.
  • Forward yield 1.95%
  • Payable July 31; for shareholders of record June 30; ex-div June 30.
  • The board approved an update to its dividend policy in anticipation of the closing of the Lexmark transaction. In December 2024, Xerox announced a reduction to its dividend in conjunction with the planned acquisition of Lexmark, reflecting the prioritization of debt repayment following acquisition close. Since then, yields on Xerox publicly traded debt have risen, resulting in an increased cost of capital and placing greater value on the reduction of debt.
  • Xerox reiterates its 2025 guidance and continues to expect the Lexmark transaction to be de-levering upon transaction close and immediately accretive to adjusted earnings per share and free cash flow . The company continues to expect synergies associated with the Lexmark transaction of at least $238 million, realizable within two years.
  • The board also declared a quarterly dividend of $20.00 per share on the outstanding Xerox Holdings Series A Convertible Perpetual Preferred Stock. The dividend is payable on July 1, 2025, to shareholders of record on June 15, 2025. 
  • See XRX Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Friday's Economic Calendar
data.description:
9:35 AM Northwest Arkansas Fireside Chat 
 
Federal Reserve Bank of St. Louis President Alberto Musalem and Federal Reserve Bank of Kansas City President Jeffrey Schmid will participate in a discussion on the economy and monetary policy before the Northwest Arkansas Fireside Chat hosted by the Federal Reserve Bank of St. Louis.
 
10:00 AM New Home Sales 
 
New home sales measure the number of newly constructed homes with a committed sale during the month. 
 
Sales expected down to 700K rate in April from 724K in March, reflecting poor affordability and consumer caution.
 
12:00 PM Fed's Cook Speech 
 
Federal Reserve Board Governor Lisa Cook will speak on"Financial Stability" before virtual Seventh Annual Women in Macro Conference, co-sponsored by New York University's Graduate School of Arts and Science, New York University's Stern School of Business, and the University of Chicago's Becker Friedman Institute.
 
1:00 PM Baker Hughes Rig Count 
 
The Baker Hughes North American rig count tracks weekly changes in the number of active operating oil & gas rigs.

data.title: Phillips 66's Los Angeles refinery layoffs to begin in December - Reuters
data.description:
Phillips 66 (NYSE:PSX) is expected to lay off most workers at its 139K bbl/day Los Angeles-area refinery in December, Reuters reported Thursday, after the company had said recently it would close the facility and begin winding down operations in October.

A few retained workers will be transferred to Phillips 66's (NYSE:PSX) Los Angeles marine oil terminal, according to the report.

Valero Energy also plans to close its 145K bbl/day Benicia refinery, one of its two remaining refineries in California, on concerns that the refining business in the state will face increasing challenges.

The two refineries that are set to shut down produce ~20% of California's gasoline supply.

data.title: Nuclear names surge as Trump set to sign orders to boost industry
data.description:
Nuclear stocks trade higher post-market Thursday after Reuters reported that President Trump will sign executive orders by as soon as tomorrow that aim to jumpstart the nuclear energy industry by easing the regulatory process on approvals for new reactors and strengthening fuel supply chains.

A draft summary of the orders said Trump will invoke the Cold War-era Defense Production Act to declare a national emergency over U.S. dependence on Russia and China for enriched uranium, nuclear fuel processing and advanced reactor inputs, according to the report.

The summary reportedly also encourages the U.S. Department of Energy to use loan guarantees and direct loans to increase the buildout of reactors; Trump only used the Loan Programs Office in his first administration to support a large nuclear plant in Georgia.

Among strong gainers after-hours: Centrus Energy (NYSE:LEU) +21.9%, Lightbridge (LTBR) +21.9%, Oklo (OKLO) +18.2%, Energy Fuels (UUUU) +17.8%, NuScale Power (NYSE:SMR) +16.3%, Uranium Energy (UEC) +14.2%, Cameco (CCJ) +7.4%, NexGen Energy (NXE) +6.8%, Denison Mines (DNN) +6.6%, Constellation Energy (CEG) +6%, Talen Energy (TLN) +3.6%, Vistra (VST) +3.3%.

ETFs: (URA), (NLR), (URNM)

data.title: Autodesk outlines $6.925B–$6.995B revenue target for fiscal 2026 as cloud and AI strategies accelerate platform growth
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Autodesk (ADSK) Q1 2026

Management View

  • CEO Andrew Anagnost stated that "Autodesk delivered strong first quarter results. Revenue and non-GAAP earnings per share topped the higher end of our guidance ranges, and billings, non-GAAP margins, and free cash flow exceeded our expectations." He emphasized the company's focus on "growth investments on our strategic priorities in cloud, platform, and AI," and remarked on optimizing sales and marketing to drive higher margins. Anagnost highlighted that new board appointments are intended to guide the next decade of growth and noted that the new transaction model is designed to drive additional business and margin opportunities.
  • Anagnost underscored three "sources of certainty" for Autodesk: the new transaction model, rebuilding free cash flow after transitioning billing, and greater transparency as the company completes its business model transitions. He cited free cash flow stack rebuilding as increasing capacity for share repurchases.
  • CFO Janesh Moorjani reported that "Total revenue in the first quarter grew 15% as reported and 16% in constant currency." The new transaction model contributed $78 million to revenue. Billings increased 29% as reported, with the new transaction model adding $105 million. Moorjani also noted, "RPO of $7.2 billion and current RPO of $4.6 billion grew 21% and 16%, respectively."
  • Moorjani stated, "First quarter GAAP and non-GAAP operating margins were 14% and 37%, respectively," explaining that GAAP margins were impacted by $105 million in restructuring charges and a $54 million non-cash charge related to stock-based compensation. He described ongoing efforts to bring stock-based compensation below 10% of revenue, now tied to executive incentives.
  • Moorjani announced, "We purchased approximately 1.3 million shares for $353 million at an average price of approximately $269 per share."

Outlook

  • Moorjani provided updated guidance: "The billings guidance range increases to $7.16 billion to $7.31 billion. The revenue guidance range increases to $6.925 billion to $6.995 billion, and the free cash flow guidance range increases to $2.1 billion to $2.2 billion."
  • Management indicated that the lower ends of billings and free cash flow guidance assume new business growth decelerates at a rate similar to the pandemic period and that EBA renewal uplift rates deteriorate. The underlying revenue growth guidance is unchanged due to its ratable nature and Q1 performance.

Financial Results

  • Total revenue for the first quarter grew 15% as reported and 16% in constant currency, with a $78 million contribution from the new transaction model. Billings increased 29% as reported, reflecting the shift to annual billings and the new transaction model.
  • RPO reached $7.2 billion, and current RPO was $4.6 billion, reflecting 21% and 16% growth, respectively. First quarter free cash flow was $556 million.
  • First quarter GAAP operating margin was 14%, impacted by restructuring and a non-cash stock-based compensation charge. Non-GAAP operating margin increased to 37%, reflecting cost discipline and timing benefits from restructuring.

Q&A

  • Saket Kalia, Barclays, asked about evolving customer sentiment amid uncertainty. Anagnost responded that "trade policy uncertainty... increases the costs of goods... and creates uncertainty in their bidding processes," but customers discuss concerns primarily for the second half of the year. He added, "Most of our customers continue to see an inflow of business."
  • Kalia inquired about margin momentum and sales/marketing optimization. Moorjani replied, "Q1 margin strength mainly was driven by the revenue outperformance, combined with just ongoing expense discipline... So far, I'd say, we are on track with the overall sales and marketing optimization plan for the year."
  • Jay Vleeschhouwer, Griffin Securities, asked about accelerated development in industry clouds and integration of ACC with Forma. Anagnost explained, "We're accelerating the roadmap around data management issues... reallocate funds and focus on these customer priorities." Regarding channel consolidation, Anagnost noted, "We want fewer, more solution-focused channel partners... we saw some fairly robust activity on the store, and the store grew quite nicely."
  • Adam Borg, Stifel, asked about progress in the transaction model and whether it was becoming a tailwind. Anagnost stated, "We're definitely not seeing the kind of disruptions we saw last year... channel productivity should increase as we head out into the year."
  • Elizabeth Porter, Morgan Stanley, questioned AI opportunities. Anagnost said, "We're very interested in delivering highly specific AI enhancements that drive real productivity gains for our customers... In Fusion land, we intend to make Fusion the most AI powered tool out there in the mechanical design market."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts displayed a neutral to slightly positive tone, seeking clarification on long-term guidance, margin momentum, and AI strategy. They pressed for more detail on macroeconomic prudence, sales optimization, and transaction model effects.
  • Management maintained a confident and measured tone in both prepared remarks and responses. Phrases such as "we are very pleased with the strong performance" and "we feel good about where we stand" reinforced a tone of stability and confidence, with prudent caution regarding macro uncertainty.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, both analysts and management maintained a consistent tone, though current quarter exchanges focused more on the pace and impact of strategic transitions and AI.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • The revenue growth rate increased to 15% as reported in Q1 versus 12% in Q4, with a larger $78 million contribution from the new transaction model in Q1 compared to $46 million in Q4.
  • Billings growth was higher in Q1 (29% as reported) versus Q4 (24% at constant currency). Non-GAAP operating margin held steady at 37% quarter-over-quarter.
  • Management's tone remained confident and focused on execution, with the current quarter highlighting progress in transaction model adoption and easing friction, whereas the previous quarter emphasized the initiation of restructuring and optimization plans.
  • Analyst focus shifted this quarter to the practical outcomes of the sales and marketing optimization and its impact on future growth, while last quarter concentrated more on the medium-term growth framework and guidance conservatism.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited heightened macroeconomic uncertainty, trade policy risks, and foreign exchange fluctuations.
  • Anagnost remarked on labor constraints in the industry, stating, "There's not enough labor, materials and capital to build and rebuild everything that needs to be rebuilt."
  • Moorjani explained that increased prudence in guidance was due to "the greater macroeconomic uncertainty," but noted, "We haven't actually seen anything" impacting business momentum yet.
  • Channel partners are still adapting to the new transaction model, but major disruptions have subsided.

Final Takeaway

Autodesk management highlighted a strong start to fiscal 2026, driven by double-digit revenue and billings growth, effective transition to a new transaction model, and progress in cloud, platform, and AI strategic priorities. Updated guidance reflects increased billings and revenue targets as well as prudent macroeconomic caution, while operational discipline and sales and marketing optimization remain central to sustaining margin expansion and shareholder value.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: StepStone outlines 37% core FRE margin target while expanding private wealth platform to $8B
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: StepStone Group Inc. (STEP) Q4 2025

Management View

  • CEO Scott Hart highlighted record quarterly and annual results, stating "Our fee related earnings, FRE margin and adjusted net income per share were all at our highest levels ever for both our quarterly and annual results." He reported over $31 billion in assets raised during the year and $27.5 billion of growth in fee earning AUM, with a 29% organic growth rate in fiscal 2025, marking the company's best 12-month period since going public.
  • Hart emphasized expansion in private wealth, noting assets grew from $3.4 billion to over $8 billion year-over-year, driven by new products and expanded distribution. He added, "We expanded our distribution partners from roughly 300 unique platforms a year ago to almost 500 platforms today, and we continue to expand our offerings outside the U.S."
  • The CEO described strong fundraising momentum, with total gross inflows of $9.9 billion in the quarter, including $1.2 billion of evergreen subscriptions—the best private wealth quarter to date. Fee earning AUM reached over $121 billion, up $7.2 billion from the prior quarter.
  • Hart noted changing market conditions: "The backdrop obviously shifted in April and has seemingly shifted back in May with rapidly evolving global trade policy driving volatility in the public markets and creating widening bid ask spreads in the private markets." He added, "We expect that we will continue to operate in an environment characterized by uncertainty."
  • Mike McCabe, Head of Strategy, detailed over $31 billion gross AUM inflows for the year, with managed accounts and commingled funds contributing $21 billion and $10 billion, respectively. Notable fund closes included $1.2 billion for the real estate secondaries fund and $300 million for the growth equity fund. McCabe reported that the real estate secondaries fund finished at over $3.75 billion, "the largest real estate secondaries fund ever raised in the industry."
  • President and Co-COO Jason Ment discussed private wealth strategy, stating, "We now offer evergreen funds across credit, infrastructure, venture and growth equity, and of course our all private markets SPRIM Fund."
  • CFO David Park stated, "We earned fee revenues of $215 million, up 40% from the prior year quarter." Park added, "Fee related earnings were $94 million, up 85% from a year ago. FRE margin was 44% for the quarter, up more than 1,000 basis points versus the prior year quarter."

Outlook

  • Park said, "I think that 37% margin is a fair expectation as a starting point. But as you know, from quarter-to-quarter, the margins are going to vary, particularly the next quarter our merit increases took effect April 1. So you should see a slight bump up in compensation as well as incremental hirings."
  • Hart noted, "We are actually feeling fairly positive in terms of the pipeline of new opportunities, whether through RFPs that we are in the process of responding to...I would say that we continue to be pleased with the amount of opportunity that lies ahead of us."

Financial Results

  • StepStone reported a GAAP net loss attributable to the company of $18.5 million or ($0.24) per share. Fee related earnings reached $94.1 million, with an FRE margin of 44%. Retroactive fees contributed $15.7 million to fee revenues. Adjusted net income for the quarter was $80.6 million or $0.68 per share.
  • For the full year, dividends declared totaled $1.36 per share, up from $0.99 last year. The quarter included a base quarterly dividend of $0.24 and a supplemental dividend of $0.40, both payable June 30.
  • Net accrued carry finished the quarter at $738 million, down 1% from last quarter but up 16% over 12 months. The quarter also saw gross realized performance fees of $81 million and net realized performance fees of $42 million, both record levels.

Q&A

  • Ken Worthington, JPMorgan: Asked about one-time fees and margin expectations. Park replied, the quarter included about $4 million of one-time advisory fees and, after adjustments, "the FRE margin for the quarter would have been 37% versus the 40% just purely excluding retroactive fees."
  • Worthington followed up on pipeline visibility. Hart responded, "We are actually feeling fairly positive in terms of the pipeline of new opportunities...we probably don't have the same size of re-up opportunities coming, but still a very healthy re-up pipeline and expect to continue to execute on that at a very high re-up rate."
  • Ben Budish, Barclays: Asked about fundraising cadence and fee rates. Hart agreed with the "barbell" fundraising pattern and noted, "In terms of the flagship funds that are currently in market today, we've mentioned in the past that our private equity co-investment fund is back in market, have not had a first closing there yet."
  • Budish also inquired about fee rates. Park answered, "If you strip out the retroactive fees, it was about 94 basis points for the quarter. But again, that is a little bit elevated, I think, because you have to factor in timing of the close."
  • Michael Cyprys, Morgan Stanley: Asked about the secondaries marketplace and scaling. Hart said, "Do expect to see increased selling in the secondaries market...we are actively evaluating opportunities in this market," but "now clearly expect that to slow down a bit just given bid ad spread and likely delay in deals given the uncertainty in the market."
  • Cyprys also asked about private wealth platform evolution. Ment said, "The U.S. market continues to be a strong one for us and the European market continues to be a lot of white space as we continue to build-out a sales force as well as syndicate partners there."
  • Chris Kotowski, Oppenheimer: Inquired about the NCI buyout. McCabe stated, "We also issued $161 million of equity. So that was 3.2 million issued shares."
  • Unidentified Analyst, Goldman Sachs: Asked about retail distribution channels and competition. Ment said, "our allocation amongst the different channels is actually fairly consistent period-over-period...despite that competition, we've posted a couple of quarters in a row of best quarters ever."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts focused on margin sustainability, fundraising outlook, and evolving fee structures, with a generally neutral to slightly positive tone, seeking clarity on recurring earnings and growth pipeline.
  • Management's tone was confident during prepared remarks and constructive in Q&A, emphasizing growth and operational strength. Park's comments included, "I think that 37% margin is a fair expectation as a starting point," showing measured optimism.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management was more assertive about record performance, while analysts maintained a consistent approach, probing for details on margin trends and fundraising cadence.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Fee related earnings increased from $74.1 million in Q3 to $94.1 million in Q4, with the FRE margin rising from 39% to 44% (37% core FRE margin, normalized for one-time and retroactive fees).
  • Private wealth AUM rose from $6 billion at the end of Q3 to over $8 billion in Q4, supported by expanded distribution.
  • Management reiterated a strong fundraising pipeline, while noting increased market volatility and scenario planning for asset allocation.
  • Analysts continued to focus on margin normalization, fee rate trends, and the sustainability of fundraising flows, echoing themes from the prior quarter.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited "rapidly evolving global trade policy driving volatility in the public markets and creating widening bid ask spreads in the private markets."
  • The outlook highlighted ongoing uncertainty, with Hart stating the company will focus on "scenario planning to quickly and dynamically assess the impact by asset class, strategy, region and sector."
  • Analysts raised concerns about sustainability of margins after normalization for one-time items, as well as visibility and timing of fundraising and deployment, especially amid market volatility.

Final Takeaway

StepStone delivered record quarterly and annual results, driven by robust asset growth, exceptional fundraising, and expansion of the private wealth platform. The company achieved its highest-ever fee related earnings and margins, while setting expectations for a normalized core FRE margin around 37% going forward. Management remains optimistic about its fundraising pipeline and the opportunities ahead, while acknowledging increased market volatility and a focus on disciplined scenario planning to navigate the current environment.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Oil prices extend losses as OPEC+ said to assess another production hike
data.description:
Crude oil prices settled lower for a third straight session on Thursday, as the possibility of another large production increase by OPEC+ for July added to concerns that global supply could outpace demand growth.

Another increase of 411K bbl/day for July is among the options under discussion ahead of the group's planned meeting on June 1, although no final agreement has been reached, Bloomberg reported, citing delegates.

A substantial production increase could exacerbate concerns of an oversupplied market, particularly as uncertainties surrounding trade talks and the broader economic outlook continue to cloud sentiment.

The speculation follows a report this week that oil production by OPEC member Kazakhstan has increased 2% this month, as the country continues to defy pressure from the cartel on reducing its output; Kazakhstan's oil production fell 3% in April, although still exceeding its OPEC+ quota.

OPEC likely made its decision to speed up production increases in part to punish members that were not complying with curbs, and could contribute to a continuation of the policy.

Geopolitics "remain pivotal for the next move in oil up to $70 Brent, or down to the $50s," Citi analysts wrote, pointing to "highly binary geopolitical risks" around U.S. negotiations on Iran's nuclear program and the Russia-Ukraine war.

Citi sees OPEC+ holding output at June levels through this year's H2, "but another three-month slug could put pressure back on the market," but meanwhile, the bank maintained its three-month Brent crude forecast at $60/bbl.

Oil prices fell in the previous session after data showed U.S. crude and fuel inventories showed surprise stock builds last week as crude imports hit a six-week high and gasoline and distillate demand slipped.

Front-month Nymex crude (CL1:COM) for July delivery finished -0.6% to $61.20/bbl, and front-month Brent crude (CO1:COM) for July delivery ended -0.7% to $64.44/bbl, the lowest settlement for both benchmarks since May 9.

Meanwhile, front-month June Nymex natural gas (NG1:COM) closed -3.4% at $3.253/MMBtu, as a large increase in underground storage - the biggest so far this injection season - confirms still soft seasonal demand; while not a surprise, the inventory build added some downward pressure to forward pricing, analysts said.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:USO), (BNO), (UCO), (SCO), (USL), (DBO), (DRIP), (GUSH), (USOI), (UNG), (BOIL), (KOLD), (UNL), (FCG)
